subtopic: Zen as pure direct experience, rejecting all systematic elaboration
text: Zen is not a systematic explanation of life, it is not an ideology, it is not a world view. The chief characteristic of Zen is that it rejects all systematic elaborations in order to get back as far as possible to the pure, unarticulated and unexplained ground of direct experience. Zen is not an explanation at all about life or existence. It is an experience, not an explanation. It is the greatest mystique, but it is not said because it cannot be said. Because it is never said, one might think it is not a mystique. Zen maintains this secrecy about what you will discover so that you approach absolutely clean and fresh. The mind has the capacity to create a hallucination of any idea. If you have any idea what you are going to find, your mind creates a dream. Then the idea becomes a reality to you -- not the real reality, but a projected one.
source: The Zen Manifesto ~ 02

subtopic: Mind as the fundamental barrier to understanding
text: Mind is misunderstanding -- any kind of mind, good or bad, educated or uneducated, cultured or uncultured, Christian or Hindu; it does not matter what kind of mind it is. Mind as such is misunderstanding. Mind means you are carrying a priori conclusions; you are not seeing that which is, you are seeing that which you want to see. The mind is a barrier. It does not allow you to see, to feel, to know, to understand. It goes on creating misunderstanding, it is the source of all distortions. Hence, unless mind is put aside, understanding does not arise. Understanding means a state of no-mind. That's what meditation is all about. Meditation is the art of putting the mind aside, not allowing it to interfere. Understanding is a byproduct of meditation; misunderstanding is a shadow of the mind.
source: Zen The Special Transmission ~ 02

subtopic: Living in the present as the only doorway to reality
text: That's the difference here. I am not telling you Hinduism is right or Christianity is right or Judaism is right. I am simply telling you mind is wrong and no-mind is right. But we all live in our prejudices because we are all past-oriented. Whatsoever had been taught to us we go on repeating, whatsoever has been told to us we will go on telling to our children. To live in the present is the only way to be really alive and to be in tune with reality. God is always present, never past, never future. No-mind is: mind never is. Either it belongs to the past or it belongs to some modified form of the past in the future. You can look in, you can just try to find out. I am not talking about any abstract theory, I am simply stating a fact.
source: Zen The Special Transmission ~ 02

subtopic: The ecstasy of silent witnessing in the present
text: If you start looking silently at things, the roses will become your ecstasy, the mountains will become your ecstasy, the naked tree without any leaves will become your ecstasy. The ecstasy is an utter silence, watching, witnessing the tremendous beauty that surrounds you. This beauty is alcoholic. Experiencing this beauty around you, you forget about small things. Your life becomes a golden life. Maneesha has asked: is our original face that of the witness? Is it that we live as amnesiacs, forgetting that we have only arbitrarily adopted a body and mind, and that enlightenment is simply regaining our memory? Yes, Maneesha. You have condensed in your question the whole answer. This is your very life source. Go deep into it, it will refresh you, rejuvenate you. It will bring freedom to you from all the nonsense that societies have poured into your mind.
source: Kyozan A True Man of Zen ~ 01

subtopic: Bliss arrives as a byproduct of living purely in the moment
text: Try to understand your ambitiousness; your effort to be somebody in the world will drive you mad. Just be nobody and then there is no problem. Drop ambitiousness and start living, because the ambitious person cannot live; he always postpones. His real life will always be tomorrow -- and the tomorrow never comes. The ambitious person is bound to be aggressive and violent, and the violent person and aggressive person are bound to go mad. The non-ambitious person is peaceful, loving, compassionate. The non-ambitious person lives herenow, and to be herenow is to be sane. To be totally in this moment is to be sane. My whole effort here is to help you become more aware of things. And the miracle of awareness is that whatsoever is wrong, the moment you become fully aware of it drops on its own accord. Only once in a while there has been a sane person -- a Buddha, a Zarathustra, a Lao Tzu, a Jesus. And the strangest thing is that these sane people look mad. Sanity means a state of peace, harmony, joy, blissfulness, benediction.
source: Zen The Special Transmission ~ 02

subtopic: The ordinariness of Zen and the relaxed quality of awakening
text: The traditional Zen is hard. It takes twenty to thirty years of constant meditation, which is unnecessary in our time. It is simply an art, to relax into yourself. The awakening of the buddha is a very easy and relaxed phenomenon. Buddhahood came to him in a relaxed state. Zen prevents you from nothing. It opens everything that is potential in you. Zazen means just sitting at the very source, not moving anywhere. Just being is Zazen. When the energy is just at the center, pulsating -- that is the very meaning of Zazen. Enlightenment is such a transformation that you are a totally different person. Once you have known it, once you have tasted it, you become silent. That which can be measured is matter, and that which cannot be measured is also real, but it is consciousness. You don't have to work for twelve years; you can just start from the relaxed state. All sutras, all scriptures are pointing to the moon -- just fingers. Don't cling to the finger; forget the finger.
source: The Zen Manifesto ~ 11

subtopic: Zen as evolution beyond perfection and all conceptual limits
text: Zen is evolution, endless evolution. Perfection is the dead end of the road, there is no more to go. A man of Zen is basically rebellious. Thomas Merton was not rebellious; he was a very obedient person. He could not live the direct experience he was talking about. He would have been the first Zen master in the West, but he was prevented by the Catholic church. Zen is beyond the mind. All categories belong to the mind. This is the only rebellion against mind: going beyond it. God is a fiction. Shunyata is the ultimate experience. You are the only problem. If you can dissolve your problem, it means you have to dissolve yourself. Nothingness is good. You will disappear in both the cases. Dispersing yourself is a beautiful experience. You are saying that we are not after anything. The unlimited also becomes to the mind just the opposite of the limited -- again, another duality.
source: The Zen Manifesto ~ 02

subtopic: The master speaks and the disciple listens with the total being
text: ISAN REPLIED, 'BY MEANS OF THE SUBTLETY OF THOUGHTLESS THOUGHT, CONTEMPLATE THE BOUNDLESS SPIRITUAL BRIGHTNESS.' Kyozan upon hearing this became enlightened. He heard this statement with his total being, not only with his ears. Underneath, something else is happening. While the master is speaking, the disciple is not only listening because he has ears. The disciple is listening with his total being, every fiber of his being; not only with ears, he is also listening with his eyes, looking at the master; he is also feeling the master, his vibe. It is a total phenomenon. He has forgotten himself and disappeared in the tremendous statement. Eastern history does not bother about physical appearance; it takes care of your spiritual growth. Always move into unity with the cosmos. Dichotomy is division; undichotomy is no division, of any kind.
source: Kyozan A True Man of Zen ~ 01

subtopic: Words are echoes; Zen's language requires spontaneity and courage
text: Zen has no fixed teaching because every fixed teaching will become out of date tomorrow. Zen is rebellious at all possible points. All words are only echoes of faraway truths. Don't cling to the echo; otherwise, who is going to discover the truth? Words are incapable of containing the truth; some other way has to be found. That some other way becomes meditation. Meditation is not a dialogue. Meditation is to get out of the mind and slowly enter into the oceanic reality. In your enlightened being you have become one with the universe. You are no more. A consciousness harmonious with existence is under the care of existence. You have said to existence, 'Now it is up to you.' Zen has a language of its own. Most particularly, it needs courage, spontaneity, clarity, and then out of that clarity whatever you do is right.
source: Kyozan A True Man of Zen ~ 02

subtopic: Using up the mind of the three worlds is nirvana
text: According to Gautam Buddha -- and confirmed by all the awakened ones -- we are divided into three parts: the body, the mind, the being. And every part has its own mind. Even the body has its own mind. Without the body having its own mind you will not be able to live a single moment, because so much work is going on inside you. When the dying master said, 'IF YOU USE UP THE MIND OF THE THREE WORLDS, THAT IS NIRVANA' -- if you can create a synchronicity between your mind, your being and the body, you have come to your fulfillment.
source: Kyozan A True Man of Zen ~ 03

subtopic: Zen's whole vision: everything is in its right place
text: Zen's whole vision is: everything is exactly in its right place. You just have to relax. You don't have to do anything to make a better world; it is your activity which is making it worse every day. Lazy people cannot be Genghis Khan killing thirty million people. They cannot be Tamerlane killing forty million people. They cannot be Nadirshah, they cannot be Alexander the Great, they cannot be Napoleon; they cannot be Ivan the Terrible, they cannot be Stalins, Mussolinis, Adolf Hitlers. You may not have seen an authentic lazy man, but he is sitting before you. I have never done anything, so the question of good and bad does not arise.
source: Kyozan A True Man of Zen ~ 04

subtopic: Understanding arises out of silence; a simple ordinary man becomes a buddha
text: Kyozan was a very simple man -- not the philosophic kind, not a poet, nor a sculptor. Zen is full of unique masters, but Kyozan's uniqueness is his simplicity. He is just like a child. Isan, it seems, took it as a challenge: if an ordinary man like Kyozan cannot be transformed into a buddha, how can you allow others to trust in the existence of the buddha? Choosing Kyozan as his successor, and waiting for forty years -- what patience! -- almost transforming a stone into a diamond. But Isan was determined to make one point absolutely clear to humanity: if Kyozan, a simple and ordinary person, not belonging to any speciality, any category, without any talent, any genius -- if he can become enlightened, it will be a proof. Isan said to his other disciples, 'Today he is not ready, he is just a seed. But tomorrow you will know why I have chosen him. If this man can become a buddha, then the doors are open for all men.'
source: Kyozan A True Man of Zen ~ 01

subtopic: The invisible gift: what Ryusen gave was material, what he received was spiritual
text: There are things which you can see, and there are things far superior, far more precious, that you cannot see. Ryusen has given only a small part of his food, but the strange monk showered him with all his joy and blissfulness, with all his light and love -- which are not visible to the ordinary eye. What Ryusen gave was material, anybody can see it. But what he received is immaterial, it is spiritual. You can see it only if your inner eye is open. You should be as humble as a beggar. You should be humble to see how much existence is pouring out for your growth.
source: Kyozan A True Man of Zen ~ 03

subtopic: The master waits forty years to transmit through a single event
text: ISAN REPLIED, 'BY MEANS OF THE SUBTLETY OF THOUGHTLESS THOUGHT -- when the mind is thoughtless and just empty, that is the temple of the buddha -- CONTEMPLATE THE BOUNDLESS SPIRITUAL BRIGHTNESS.' The disciple is listening with his total being, every fiber of his being; not only with ears, he is also listening with his eyes, looking at the master; he is also feeling the master, his vibe. It is a total phenomenon. He has forgotten himself and disappeared in the tremendous statement. The master declares your potential buddhahood, and unless your potential buddhahood is declared you may never think that you are a buddha. Enlightenment is such a subtle fragrance, so invisible a flower that only another enlightened person can recognize it.
source: Kyozan A True Man of Zen ~ 01

subtopic: Ego dissolved through humor: nothing hurts the ego more than a joke
text: The relationship is like an arch. When you make an arch you put bricks against each other; their very opposition creates the strength, and the arch can support the whole building. But the strength depends on the opposition. A living relationship between a man and a woman is bound to be a little bit crazy. Man cannot drive the woman crazy because his argument, his way of thinking is logical. The woman's way of thinking is illogical, but that is her way; that's how she is made. She functions instinctively at the lowest and intuitively at the highest. Man functions intellectually at the lowest and intelligently at the highest. The way of instinct and intuition is the way of illogic. Nothing can hurt the ego more deeply than a joke. It goes like an arrow and it goes in such a subtle way that there is no protection against it.
source: Zen The Special Transmission ~ 04

subtopic: Awareness is alchemical: what is wrong drops on its own accord
text: IT IS GOOD, it is beautiful that you recognize, that you confess that you are a hypocrite. This is the beginning -- the beginning of sincerity, the beginning of truth. Recognition begins the mask's slipping; hypocrisy survives only through denial. When people deceive others repeatedly, they eventually deceive themselves, forgetting authentic language. Wearing masks so long transforms them into faces -- almost welded to one's identity. Being with a Master is to be on a surgical table, involving painful removal of accumulated falseness that has become second nature. My whole effort here is to help you become more aware of things. And the miracle of awareness is that whatsoever is wrong, the moment you become fully aware of it drops on its own accord.
source: Zen The Special Transmission ~ 02

subtopic: Poverty of the inner world: no ego, no pride, pure humbleness
text: I call you 'poor Maneesha' because there is a poverty of the outer world, and there is a poverty of the inner world. I am against the poverty of the outer world, but I am not against the poverty of the inner world. The poverty of the inner world means: no ego, no pride, no arrogance, pure humbleness, as if you are not. And what begins 'as if' soon you find it is so. There is no need to call it 'as if.' The real poverty means the ego has shattered, ambition has left you. Such deep poverty is really great richness.
source: Kyozan A True Man of Zen ~ 01

subtopic: Satori as nothingness: pure witnessing buddha remains
text: Suddenly a door opens into the cosmos. You see from where your life has been coming. You see you are no more, only existence is. This pure nothingness, this shunyata is the only religious experience there is. Christianity is the only religion in the world which is becoming aware that their congregation is disappearing -- they are all going to Poona! In resurrection you will be men of Zen. Deepen your witnessing through relaxation and dissolution into consciousness. This auditorium has become an ocean of consciousness. Ten thousand buddhas are disappearing into one oceanic feeling, into one oceanic experience. Only a pure witnessing buddha remains, utterly transparent, so he cannot make any shadow. Go more and more into your being, your center. Feel the very source of your life -- pulsating, throbbing, alive.
source: The Zen Manifesto ~ 02

subtopic: The moment of enlightenment arrives through total receptivity
text: Ryusen moved from master to master with an open heart, with a receptivity, with a begging bowl, and finally he found Kyozan. And it is a strange experience, when you find your master; deep in your heart so many bells start ringing, so much dance, every cell of your being becomes alive. And when he found Kyozan, the road came to an end. Then he remained with Kyozan, and became enlightened. He established a new monastery in the mountains. This has been a Zen tradition, that at the last moment disciples ask the master whatever is the most prominent question in their mind, because the master will not be available any more.
source: Kyozan A True Man of Zen ~ 03

subtopic: Meditation as the only thing death cannot destroy
text: And remember, you have only one energy; you don't have many energies, only one energy. At the lowest it is called sex energy. You go on refining it, you go on transforming it through meditation, through the alchemy of meditation, and the same energy starts moving upwards. It becomes love, it becomes prayer. It is the same energy, just refined states of it. Sex is crude, raw, just a diamond found from the mines. It has to be cut, polished; much work is needed. Then it will be possible to recognize that it is a diamond. It is only meditation that gives you something which death cannot destroy, which is indestructible. Meditation reveals to you your Buddhahood. It will make you a Christ, a Zarathustra, a Lao Tzu.
source: Zen The Special Transmission ~ 04

subtopic: In deep meditation the lover finds the beloved as one
text: Truth needs guts and courage. It comes out of a loving search. The inquirer is a lover; he is trying to find the ultimate lover. Even the ordinary love affairs are stepping-stones. But love is in bondage. The society has sealed every love. Anything that becomes a law becomes dead. When love becomes a marriage you are committing suicide. If love remains a freedom, step-by-step you will be able to reach your innermost being. It is saying that if you go deep in meditation a moment comes when the lover finds the beloved -- not as a separate entity, but as one with himself. One is both: the lover and the loved. This non-duality takes you to the highest peak of consciousness.
source: Kyozan A True Man of Zen ~ 03

subtopic: Zen proclaims your god is your innermost being
text: Zen is religion and is not religion. It is religion in the sense that it brings you to your godliness. Zen proclaims your god is your innermost being, and the moment you reach to your innermost being all your personality disappears as if it was a dream, and now you are awake. Words have to be discarded by words themselves, just as poison has to be destroyed by more poison. In this small anecdote is contained the whole of Zen. You have to drop all that has been given to you by others. When only a witness remains which you cannot drop, you have arrived home. Zen has brought religion to its scientific crescendo.
source: Kyozan A True Man of Zen ~ 04

subtopic: Zen's non-competitive spirit: sleeping and awake buddhas are not different
text: Zen's whole vision is: existence accepts everything. No competitive spirit exists. No master ranks above another; enlightened ones are not superior to unenlightened ones -- merely in different states. Being awake differs from sleep but involves no superiority claims. The moment an experience becomes an explanation, a philosophy, a theology, it loses life. Zen is both a religion and a revolution against religion; this makes people confused. The function of meditation is to bring your childhood back to you. In your enlightened being you have become one with the universe. You are no more. A consciousness harmonious with existence is under the care of existence.
source: Kyozan A True Man of Zen ~ 02

subtopic: A man who meditates deeply passes the door of death many times
text: A man who has been going deeper into meditation passes the door of death many times. Whenever he goes, he passes it, whenever he comes back -- it is simple to understand that death has nothing to do with life. And out of fear -- it is a chain -- other things will arise: out of fear you will believe in God; out of fear you will believe in the priest; out of fear you will go to the temple, to the mosque, to the church. Anything done out of fear is not religious. That point is absolute. Anything arising out of fear cannot lead you to truth. Ryusen said, 'IF YOU USE UP THE MIND OF THE THREE WORLDS, THAT IS NIRVANA.' Saying this he sat in the proper manner -- that is the lotus posture -- and passed away.
source: Kyozan A True Man of Zen ~ 03

subtopic: Your only identity is witnessing: the Sanskrit word for witnessing is buddha
text: This is your very life source. Go deep into it, it will refresh you, rejuvenate you. It will bring freedom to you from all the nonsense that societies have poured into your mind. This moment you are the buddha. Just witnessing, doing nothing -- the same mirror that Isan has sent to Kyozan as a gift. The mirror only witnesses. Relax and witness the body is separate from you, the mind is separate from you. Your only identity is witnessing. The Sanskrit word for witnessing is buddha. Is not this moment the most precious, because you are at home? To be a buddha simply means to be at home. The ultimate in the search for truth is to bring the hidden buddha into your day-to-day actions, gestures, words, silences.
source: Kyozan A True Man of Zen ~ 01
